116	def check_if_sentencepiece_model(model, temporary_location = "_unsloth_sentencepiece_temp"):
117	    if not hasattr(model, "_saved_temp_tokenizer"): return False
118	
119	    temp_tokenizer = model._saved_temp_tokenizer
120	    sentencepiece_model = False
121	    file_location = os.path.join(temporary_location, temp_tokenizer.name_or_path)
122	    created_folder = False
123	    if not os.path.exists(file_location):
124	        created_folder = True
125	        os.makedirs(file_location)
126	    pass
127	    temp_tokenizer.save_pretrained(file_location)
128	    if os.path.isfile(f"{file_location}/tokenizer.model"):
129	        sentencepiece_model = True
130	    pass
131	    if created_folder:
132	        shutil.rmtree(file_location, ignore_errors = True)
133	    return sentencepiece_model

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass
156	pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass
174	pass

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass
276	
277	    @staticmethod
278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)
301	        d_KA = torch.empty_like(KA)
302	        d_KB = torch.empty_like(KB)
303	        d_VA = torch.empty_like(VA)
304	        d_VB = torch.empty_like(VB)
305	
306	        # Q Projection
307	        # d_QA = X.t() @ (dQ @ QB.t())
308	        # d_QB = (QA.t() @ X.t()) @ dQ
309	        # d_QA *= QS
310	        # d_QB *= QS
311	        d_QA.addmm_(X.t(), dQ @ QB.t(), alpha = QS, beta = 0)
312	        d_QB.addmm_(QA.t() @ X.t(), dQ, alpha = QS, beta = 0)
313	
314	        # K Projection
315	        # d_KA = X.t() @ (dK @ KB.t())
316	        # d_KB = (KA.t() @ X.t()) @ dK
317	        # d_KA *= KS
318	        # d_KB *= KS
319	        d_KA.addmm_(X.t(), dK @ KB.t(), alpha = KS, beta = 0)
320	        d_KB.addmm_(KA.t() @ X.t(), dK, alpha = KS, beta = 0)
321	
322	        # V Projection
323	        # d_VA = X.t() @ (dV @ VB.t())
324	        # d_VB = (VA.t() @ X.t()) @ dV
325	        # d_VA *= VS
326	        # d_VB *= VS
327	        d_VA.addmm_(X.t(), dV @ VB.t(), alpha = VS, beta = 0)
328	        d_VB.addmm_(VA.t() @ X.t(), dV, alpha = VS, beta = 0)
329	
330	        # Combine derivatives to find dX
331	        # dQ
332	        QW = fast_dequantize(QW.t(), QW_quant)
333	        dX = torch.matmul(dQ, QW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
334	        del QW
335	        # dX += (dQ @ QB.to(dtype).t() @ (QS * QA.to(dtype).t()))
336	        dX.addmm_(dQ @ QB.t(), QA.t(), alpha = QS)
337	
338	        # dK
339	        KW = fast_dequantize(KW.t(), KW_quant)
340	        # dX += dK @ KW.t()
341	        dX.addmm_(dK, KW.t())
342	        del KW
343	        # dX += dK @ KB.to(dtype).t() @ (KS * KA.to(dtype).t())
344	        dX.addmm_(dK @ KB.t(), KA.t(), alpha = KS)
345	
346	        # dV
347	        VW = fast_dequantize(VW.t(), VW_quant)
348	        # dX += dV @ VW.t()
349	        dX.addmm_(dV, VW.t())
350	        del VW
351	        # dX += dV @ VB.to(dtype).t() @ (VS * VA.to(dtype).t())
352	        dX.addmm_(dV @ VB.t(), VA.t(), alpha = VS)
353	
354	        # QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
355	        # KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
356	        # VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
357	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
358	            None, None, d_QA.t(), d_QB.t(), None, \
359	            None, None, d_KA.t(), d_KB.t(), None, \
360	            None, None, d_VA.t(), d_VB.t(), None, \
361	            None,
362	    pass
363	pass
364

366	def apply_lora_qkv(self, X, inplace = True):
367	    X = _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X, self.q_proj)
368	    QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS = get_lora_parameters(self.q_proj)
369	    KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS = get_lora_parameters(self.k_proj)
370	    VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS = get_lora_parameters(self.v_proj)
371	    Q, K, V = LoRA_QKV.apply(X,
372	        QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
373	        KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
374	        VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
375	        inplace,
376	    )
377	    return Q, K, V

381	class LoRA_W(torch.autograd.Function):
382	    """
383	    ### LoRA weights
384	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
385	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
386	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
387	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
388	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
389	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
390	
391	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
392	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
393	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
394	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
395	
396	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
397	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
398	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
399	
400	    ### K projection LoRA weights
401	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
402	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
403	
404	    ### V projection LoRA weights
405	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
406	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
407	    """
408	    @staticmethod
409	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
410	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
411	                W, W_quant, A, B, S):
412	        dtype = X.dtype
413	        XW = matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, S)
414	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (W, W_quant, S,)
415	        ctx.save_for_backward(A, B, X)
416	        return XW
417	    pass
418	
419	    @staticmethod
420	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
421	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
422	        W, W_quant, S = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
423	        A, B, X = ctx.saved_tensors
424	
425	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
426	        dY = dY.reshape(-1, dY.shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
427	        X  = X .reshape(-1, X .shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
428	        dtype = X.dtype
429	
430	        A, B = A.to(dtype), B.to(dtype)
431	
432	        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
433	
434	        d_A = torch.empty_like(A)
435	        d_B = torch.empty_like(B)
436	
437	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
438	        # Weight projection
439	        # d_A = X.t() @ (dY @ B.t())
440	        # d_B = (A.t() @ X.t()) @ dY
441	        # d_A *= S
442	        # d_B *= S
443	        d_A.addmm_(X.t(), dY @ B.t(), alpha = S, beta = 0)
444	        d_B.addmm_(A.t() @ X.t(), dY, alpha = S, beta = 0)
445	
446	        # Get derivative for dX
447	        W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant)
448	        dX = dY @ W.t()
449	        del W
450	        # dX += dY @ B.to(dtype).t() @ (S * A.to(dtype).t())
451	        dX.addmm_(dY @ B.t(), A.t(), alpha = S)
452	
453	        # W, W_quant, A, B, S
454	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
455	            None, None, d_A.t(), d_B.t(), None
456	    pass
457	pass
458

463	    O = LoRA_W.apply(X, OW, OW_quant, OA, OB, OS)
464	    return O
465	pass

468	IDENTITY_DROPOUT = torch.nn.Identity
469	@torch._disable_dynamo
470	def fast_lora_forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, *args, **kwargs) -> torch.Tensor:
471	    raise NotImplementedError(
472	        "Unsloth: Currently not supported yet - reshaping done incorrectly"
473	    )
474	    self._check_forward_args(x, *args, **kwargs)
475	    adapter_names = kwargs.pop("adapter_names", None)
476	
477	    if self.disable_adapters:
478	        if self.merged:
479	            self.unmerge()
480	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
481	    elif adapter_names is not None:
482	        result = self._mixed_batch_forward(x, *args, adapter_names=adapter_names, **kwargs)
483	    elif self.merged:
484	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
485	    else:
486	        # Fastpath
487	        if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
488	            active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
489	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys(): return self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
490	
491	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
492	            if isinstance(dropout, IDENTITY_DROPOUT) and not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
493	                lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
494	                lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
495	                scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
496	                W = self.base_layer.weight
497	                return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
498	            pass
499	        pass
500	
501	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
502	        # As per Tim Dettmers, for 4bit, we need to defensively clone here.
503	        # The reason is that in some cases, an error can occur that backprop
504	        # does not work on a manipulated view. This issue may be solved with
505	        # newer PyTorch versions but this would need extensive testing to be
506	        # sure.
507	        result = result.clone()
508	
509	        for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
510	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
511	                continue
512	            lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
513	            lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
514	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
515	            scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
516	
517	            requires_conversion = not torch.is_autocast_enabled()
518	            if requires_conversion:
519	                expected_dtype = result.dtype
520	                x = x.to(lora_A.weight.dtype)
521	
522	            if not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
523	                result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
524	            else:
525	                if isinstance(dropout, torch.nn.Identity) or not self.training:
526	                    base_result = result
527	                else:
528	                    x = dropout(x)
529	                    base_result = None
530	
531	                result = result + self.lora_magnitude_vector[active_adapter](
532	                    x,
533	                    lora_A=lora_A,
534	                    lora_B=lora_B,
535	                    scaling=scaling,
536	                    base_layer=self.get_base_layer(),
537	                    base_result=base_result,
538	                )
539	            if requires_conversion:

136	def _free_cached_model(model):
137	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
138	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
139	
140	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
141	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
142	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
143	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
144	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
145	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass

204	@torch.inference_mode
205	def unsloth_save_model(
206	    model,
207	    tokenizer,
208	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
209	    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
210	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
211	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
212	    is_main_process      : bool = True,
213	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
214	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
215	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
216	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
217	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
218	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
219	
220	    # Push to hub
221	    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
222	    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
223	    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
224	    create_pr            : bool = False,
225	    revision             : str = None,
226	    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
227	    tags                 : List[str] = None,
228	
229	    # Our functions
230	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
231	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
232	):
233	    if token is None: token = get_token()
234	
235	    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
236	    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
237	        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
238	    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()
239	
240	    if commit_description is None:
241	        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
242	    elif "Unsloth 2x faster" not in commit_description:
243	        commit_description += " (Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
244	    pass
245	
246	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
247	        raise RuntimeError(
248	            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
249	            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
250	            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
251	            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
252	        )
253	    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
254	        save_method = "merged_4bit"
255	    pass
256	
257	    save_pretrained_settings = dict(locals())
258	    for deletion in ("model", "tokenizer", "save_method", "temporary_location", "maximum_memory_usage"):
259	        del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
260	    pass
261	
262	    # First check for a token!
263	    if push_to_hub:
264	        from huggingface_hub import whoami
265	        try:
266	            username = whoami(token = token)["name"]
267	        except:
268	            raise RuntimeError(
269	                "Unsloth: Please supply a token!\n"\
270	                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens"
271	            )
272	        pass
273	    pass
274	
275	    assert(maximum_memory_usage > 0 and maximum_memory_usage <= 0.95)
276	
277	    # Clean memory up first
278	    for _ in range(3):
279	        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
280	        gc.collect()
281	    pass
282	
283	    save_method = save_method.lower().replace(" ", "_")
284	    if save_method != "lora" and save_method != "merged_16bit" and save_method != "merged_4bit":
285	        raise RuntimeError(
286	            "Unsloth: You must select one of 3 options when saving models:\n"\
287	            '"lora"         ==> This is the fastest and easiet. Just saves LoRA modules.\n'\
288	            '"merged_16bit" ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to float16. Needed for llama.cpp / GGUF.\n'\
289	            '"merged_4bit"  ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to 4bit. Useful for DPO / inference.'
290	        )
291	    pass
292	
293	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":

64	def calculate_settings(n : int) -> (int, int,):
65	    BLOCK_SIZE : int = next_power_of_2(n)
66	    if BLOCK_SIZE > MAX_FUSED_SIZE:
67	        raise RuntimeError(f"Cannot launch Triton kernel since n = {n} exceeds "\
68	                           f"the maximum CUDA blocksize = {MAX_FUSED_SIZE}.")
69	    num_warps : int = 4
70	    if   BLOCK_SIZE >= 32768: num_warps = 32
71	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  8192: num_warps = 16
72	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  2048: num_warps = 8
73	    return BLOCK_SIZE, num_warps

2584	    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")
2585	
2586	    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
2587	    """
2588	    {docs}
2589	    """
2590	    arguments = dict(locals())
2591	    del arguments["self"]
2592	    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
2593	        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
2594	        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
2595	    elif "tags" in arguments:
2596	        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
2597	    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
2598	        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
2599	
2600	    if "commit_message" in arguments:
2601	        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
2602	        if commit_message is not None:
2603	            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
2604	            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
2605	                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
2606	        else:
2607	            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
2608	        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message
2609	
2610	    if "commit_description" in arguments:
2611	        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
2612	        if commit_description is not None:
2613	            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
2614	            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
2615	                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
2616	        else:
2617	            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
2618	        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description
2619	
2620	    # Update model tag
2621	    if hasattr(self, "config"):
2622	        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
2623	            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
2624	            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
2625	            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
2626	        )
2627	    pass
2628	
2629	    try:
2630	        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
2631	    except:
2632	        del arguments["tags"]
2633	        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
2634	    pass
2635	
2636	    if hasattr(self, "config"):
2637	        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
2638	    pass
2639	    '''
2640	    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())
2641	
2642	    original_model = model
2643	    while True:
2644	
2645	        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
2646	            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
2647	            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)

2650	            pass

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:

261	    """
262	    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.

275	if DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu" and HAS_XPU_STREAM:
276	    @torch.inference_mode
277	    def fast_dequantize(W, quant_state = None, out = None, use_global_buffer = False):
278	        # TODO: After adding XPU BNB support, check this function
279	        if quant_state is None: return W
280	        if type(quant_state) is not list:
281	            # New quant_state as a class
282	            # https://github.com/TimDettmers/bitsandbytes/pull/763/files
283	            absmax     = quant_state.absmax
284	            shape      = quant_state.shape
285	            dtype      = quant_state.dtype
286	            blocksize  = quant_state.blocksize
287	            offset     = quant_state.offset
288	            state2     = quant_state.state2
289	            absmax2    = state2.absmax
290	            code2      = state2.code
291	            blocksize2 = state2.blocksize
292	        else:
293	            # Old quant_state as a list of lists
294	            absmax, shape, dtype, blocksize, compressed_stats, _, _ = quant_state
295	            offset, state2 = compressed_stats
296	            absmax2, code2, blocksize2, _, _, _, _ = state2
297	        pass
298	        global XPU_STREAMS
299	        device = W.device
300	        device_index = device.index
301	        XPU_STREAM = XPU_STREAMS[device_index]
302	
303	        n_elements_absmax = absmax.numel()
304	        # Create weight matrix
305	        if use_global_buffer:
306	
307	            # Use same buffers for faster inference
308	            size = shape[0]*shape[1]
309	            global WEIGHT_BUFFERS
310	            global ABSMAX_BUFFERS
311	            WEIGHT_BUFFER = WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index]
312	            ABSMAX_BUFFER = ABSMAX_BUFFERS[device_index]
313	            if WEIGHT_BUFFER is None:
314	                WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index] = WEIGHT_BUFFER = torch_empty(size, dtype = dtype, device = device, requires_grad = False)
315	                ABSMAX_BUFFERS[device_index] = ABSMAX_BUFFER = torch_empty(n_elements_absmax, dtype = torch.float32, device = device, requires_grad = False)
316	
317	            if size > WEIGHT_BUFFER.numel(): WEIGHT_BUFFER.resize_(size)
318	            if n_elements_absmax > ABSMAX_BUFFER.numel(): ABSMAX_BUFFER.resize_(n_elements_absmax)
319	
320	            out = WEIGHT_BUFFER[:size].view(shape)
321	            out_absmax = ABSMAX_BUFFER[:n_elements_absmax]
322	        else:
323	            if out is None:
324	                out = torch_empty(shape, dtype = dtype, device = device, requires_grad = False)
325	            else:
326	                assert(out.shape == shape)
327	                assert(out.dtype == dtype)
328	            out_absmax = torch_empty(n_elements_absmax, dtype = torch_float32, device = device, requires_grad = False)
329	        pass

# Unsloth gradient accumulation fix:
from transformers import __version__ as transformers_version
if Version(transformers_version) > Version("4.45.2"):
    def unsloth_train(trainer, *args, **kwargs):
        return trainer.train(*args, **kwargs)
    pass
else:
    def unsloth_train(trainer, *args, **kwargs):
        if len(args) != 0 or len(kwargs) != 0:
            raise RuntimeError(
                "Unsloth: Our custom gradient accumulation fixed trainer does not support other arguments.\n"\
                "If you want to use our fix inside of HF, please update `transformers` to the latest version via:\n"\
                '`pip uninstall transformers -y && pip install --upgrade --no-cache-dir transformers`'
            )
        print(
            "Unsloth: Using our custom gradient accumulation fixed trainer, which is not feature complete.\n"\
            "If you want to use our fix inside of HF, please update `transformers` to the latest version via:\n"\
            '`pip uninstall transformers -y && pip install --upgrade --no-cache-dir transformers`'
        )
        return _unsloth_train(trainer)
    pass
pass

try:
    from trl import SFTConfig as TrainingArguments
except:
    from transformers import TrainingArguments
pass

class UnslothTrainingArguments(TrainingArguments):
    def __init__(self, embedding_learning_rate: float = None, *args, **kwargs):
        embedding_learning_rate = embedding_learning_rate
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
pass


def _create_unsloth_optimizer(
    model,
    optimizer_cls,
    optimizer_kwargs,
    embedding_lr = 5e-5,
):
    lr = optimizer_kwargs["lr"]
    weight_decay = optimizer_kwargs.get("weight_decay", 0.0)

    param_groups = \
    {
        "non_embeddings" : {},
        "embeddings"     : {},
    }

    for name, param in model.named_parameters():
        if not param.requires_grad: continue
        if name.endswith("modules_to_save.default.weight"):
            partial_name = name[:-len(".modules_to_save.default.weight")]
            partial_name = partial_name[partial_name.rfind(".")+1:]
            print(f"Unsloth: Setting lr = {embedding_lr:.2e} instead of {lr:.2e} for {partial_name}.")
            param_groups["embeddings"]    [name] = param
        else:
            param_groups["non_embeddings"][name] = param

def try_fix_tokenizer(tokenizer, prepend = True):

    if hasattr(tokenizer, "_tokenizer"):
        converted_tokenizer = tokenizer._tokenizer
    else:
        converted_tokenizer = convert_slow_tokenizer(tokenizer)
    pass

    tokenizer_string = converted_tokenizer.to_str()

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors

261	def _convert_lora_to_linear(module: LoraLayer, adapter_name: str = "default"):
262	    base_layer = module.get_base_layer()
263	    weight = base_layer.weight
264	
265	    assert isinstance(weight, bnb.nn.Params4bit)
266	    quant_state = weight.quant_state
267	    original_dtype = quant_state.dtype
268	
269	    w_dq = dequantize_4bit(weight.data, quant_state).float()
270	    lora_delta = (
271	        module.lora_B[adapter_name].weight
272	        @ module.lora_A[adapter_name].weight
273	        * module.scaling[adapter_name]
274	    )
275	    w_dq += lora_delta.float()
276	    w_dq = w_dq.to(original_dtype)
277	
278	    new_module = torch.nn.Linear(
279	        w_dq.shape[1], w_dq.shape[0], bias=module.base_layer.bias is not None
280	    )
281	    new_module.weight.data = torch.nn.Parameter(w_dq, requires_grad=False)
282	    if module.lora_bias[adapter_name]:
283	        bias_data = module.base_layer.bias.data + module.lora_B[adapter_name].bias
284	        new_module.bias.data = torch.nn.Parameter(bias_data, requires_grad=False)
285	    return new_module
286	
287	
288	def convert_lora_to_linear(model: torch.nn.Module):
289	    replace_module(model, LoraLayer, _convert_lora_to_linear)
290	    assert not any(isinstance(module, LoraLayer) for module in model.modules())
291	    return model

torch_float16  = torch.float16
torch_bfloat16 = torch.bfloat16

def QUANT_STATE(W): return getattr(W, "quant_state", None)

def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
--

def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me create a detailed report:

## Summary: Unsloth Public API and Model Saving Methods

I've thoroughly analyzed the unsloth codebase to understand the public API structure and how saving methods are exposed to users. Here are my findings:

### 1. **Main Entry Point: FastLanguageModel**

**Location:** `/unsloth/models/loader.py` (lines 107+)

`FastLanguageModel` is the primary entry point class that inherits from `FastLlamaModel`. It exposes the static method:
- `from_pretrained()` - Main factory method for loading models with LoRA/QLoRA support

**Key parameters:**
- `model_name`, `max_seq_length`, `dtype`, `load_in_4bit`, `load_in_8bit`, `load_in_16bit`, `full_finetuning`, `token`, `device_map`, `rope_scaling`, `fix_tokenizer`, `trust_remote_code`, `use_gradient_checkpointing`, `resize_model_vocab`, `revision`, `fast_inference`

Now let me create a comprehensive summary document based on all the information I've gathered:

Based on my comprehensive search of the unsloth codebase, here's a detailed report on LoRA adapter weights handling:

## Summary: LoRA Adapter Weights in Unsloth

### 1. **LoRA A and B Matrices Storage and Access**

**File**: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775505501699-4z3rxe/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-256)

The codebase accesses LoRA matrices through PEFT's standard interface:

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    # Lines 214-215:
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    # Access weights via:
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    
def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # Lines 252-254:
    proj.lora_A[adapter].weight
    proj.lora_B[adapter].weight
    proj.scaling[adapter]
```

**Access Pattern**: Layers have `lora_A` and `lora_B` as dictionaries indexed by adapter name (typically "default"). Each contains a `.weight` tensor.

def _merge_lora(layer, name):

            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)

126	        if self.optimizer is None:
127	            optimizer_cls, optimizer_kwargs = SFTTrainer.get_optimizer_cls_and_kwargs(self.args)
128	            self.optimizer = _create_unsloth_optimizer(
129	                self.model,
130	                optimizer_cls,
131	                optimizer_kwargs,
132	                embedding_learning_rate,
133	            )
134	        pass
135	        return self.optimizer
136	    pass
137	pass
138	
139	# From `trl>=0.13.0`, they changed how to pass several params to the trainer
140	# We need to patch to make the transition smooth
141	def _backwards_compatible_trainer(trainer_class, config_class):
142	    original_init = trainer_class.__init__
143	    
144	    @wraps(original_init)
145	    def new_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
146	        # All Trainer tokenizer are now called processing_class
147	        trainer_params = set(inspect.signature(original_init).parameters.keys())
148	
149	        if "processing_class" in trainer_params and "tokenizer" in kwargs:
150	            kwargs["processing_class"] = kwargs.pop("tokenizer")
151	        pass
152	
153	        if ("args" in kwargs) and (Version(trl.__version__) >= Version("0.13.0.dev0")):
154	            training_args = kwargs.pop("args", None)
155	
156	            # Get parameters that Trainer.__init__ actually expects
157	            trainer_params.remove('self')
158	            trainer_params.remove('args')
159	
160	            # Get fields that should be passed to Config init
161	            config_fields = {
162	                field.name: field for field in dataclasses.fields(config_class) 
163	                if field.init
164	            }
165	            
166	            # Create config dict with valid fields from training_args
167	            config_dict = {
168	                name: getattr(training_args, name)
169	                for name in config_fields
170	                if hasattr(training_args, name)
171	            }
172	
173	            # Get parameters that exist in Config but not in TrainingArguments
174	            from transformers import TrainingArguments
175	            moved_params = \
176	                set(inspect.signature(config_class)     .parameters.keys()) - \
177	                set(inspect.signature(TrainingArguments).parameters.keys())
178	            
179	            # Separate kwargs into trainer kwargs and config kwargs
180	            trainer_kwargs = {}
181	            additional_config_kwargs = {}
182	
183	            for key, value in kwargs.items():
184	                if key in trainer_params: trainer_kwargs[key] = value
185	                elif key in moved_params or key in config_fields:
186	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
187	                else:
188	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
189	                pass
190	            pass
191	
192	            # Update config_dict with additional kwargs
193	            config_dict.update(additional_config_kwargs)
194	
195	            # Create Config with all the collected parameters
196	            # Reinitialising config class with parameters (that were none initially but populated on first init)
197	            # causes the 2nd init to fail as there are mutual exclusive checks on pairs of parameters.
198	            # Refer: https://github.com/huggingface/trl/blob/main/trl/trainer/grpo_config.py#L499-L502 for example
199	            # So we only create config class if the previous init was not TrainingArguments
200	            if not isinstance(training_args, TrainingArguments):
201	                config = config_class(**config_dict)
202	            else:
203	                config = training_args
204	
205	            # Reconstruct kwargs for Trainer
206	            kwargs = trainer_kwargs
207	            kwargs["args"] = config
208	        pass
209	        original_init(self, *args, **kwargs)
210	    pass
211	    return new_init
212	pass
213	
214	
215	def _patch_trl_trainer():
216	    import trl
217	    if hasattr(trl, "__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__"): return
218	    if Version(trl.__version__) <= Version("0.11.0"): return
219	
220	    import trl.trainer
221	    trl_classes = dir(trl.trainer)
222	    trl_trainers = set(x[:-len("Trainer")] for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Trainer"))
223	    trl_configs  = set(x[:-len("Config")]  for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Config"))
224	    trl_classes = list(trl_trainers & trl_configs)
225	
226	    for x in trl_classes:
227	        try:    exec(f"trl.{x}Trainer.__init__ = _backwards_compatible_trainer(trl.{x}Trainer, trl.{x}Config)", globals())
228	        except: continue
229	    pass
230	
231	    trl.__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__ = True
232	pass
233

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive summary. Let me compile all my findings:

## Comprehensive Exploration of Unsloth Codebase

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's a complete understanding of the structure and how LoRA adapters are represented and exported:

### 1. TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY LAYOUT

**Root Level Structure:**
```
unsloth/
├── __init__.py                  # Main entry point, imports models, save, chat_templates, etc.
├── _auto_install.py
├── import_fixes.py
├── chat_templates.py            # 129KB - Chat template utilities
├── save.py                       # 105KB - CRITICAL: All save/export methods
├── tokenizer_utils.py
├── trainer.py                    # UnslothTrainer and training utilities
├── dataprep/                     # Data preparation utilities
├── kernels/                      # CUDA/Triton kernel implementations
│   ├── fast_lora.py            # LoRA computation kernels
│   ├── utils.py                # CRITICAL: get_lora_parameters functions
│   └── ...
├── models/                       # Main model definitions
│   ├── loader.py               # FastLanguageModel class - main API
│   ├── llama.py                # FastLlamaModel - 3036 lines
│   ├── mistral.py
│   ├── qwen2.py, qwen3.py
│   ├── gemma.py, gemma2.py
│   ├── granite.py
│   ├── cohere.py
│   ├── _utils.py               # Utility functions
│   ├── mapper.py               # Model name mappings
│   └── ...
├── registry/                     # Model registry
└── utils/                        # General utilities
```

---

### 2. MAIN MODEL CLASSES & ARCHITECTURE

**Primary Entry Point:**
- **`FastLanguageModel`** (in `/unsloth/models/loader.py`)
  - Static factory method: `from_pretrained()`
  - Inherits from `FastLlamaModel`
  - Returns: `(model, tokenizer)` tuple

**Model Type Hierarchy:**
- `FastLanguageModel` → `FastLlamaModel` → `FastBaseModel`
- Model-specific implementations: `FastMistralModel`, `FastQwen3Model`, `FastGemmaModel`, etc.

**Key Model Structure After Loading:**
- Models wrapped with PEFT (Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning) for LoRA
- Can be of type: `PeftModelForCausalLM` (from PEFT library)
- Contains nested `model` attribute for accessing internal model

**Critical Configuration Access:**
```python
# After loading a model with FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained()
if hasattr(model, "peft_config"):
    peft_config = model.peft_config["default"].to_dict()
    # Returns dict with keys: r, lora_alpha, target_modules, lora_dropout, etc.
```

---

### 3. LoRA ADAPTER REPRESENTATION

**LoRA Matrices Structure (from `unsloth/kernels/utils.py`):**

Each LoRA-enabled linear layer contains:
- **`lora_A[adapter]`** - PEFT Linear layer containing weight A matrix (shape: `[rank, input_dim]`)
- **`lora_B[adapter]`** - PEFT Linear layer containing weight B matrix (shape: `[output_dim, rank]`)  
- **`scaling[adapter]`** - Scaling factor (alpha / rank)
- **Active adapter** - Name of the active LoRA adapter (default: `"default"`)

**Access Pattern (from `kernels/utils.py` functions):**
```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """Returns (weight, quant_state, lora_A, lora_B, lora_scale)"""
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))[0]
    lora_A = proj.lora_A[adapter].weight      # Shape: [rank, in_features]
    lora_B = proj.lora_B[adapter].weight      # Shape: [out_features, rank]
    scaling = proj.scaling[adapter]            # alpha / rank
    return (W, quant_state, lora_A, lora_B, scaling)

def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    """Same as above but also returns bias"""
    # Returns (W, quant_state, lora_A, lora_B, lora_scale, bias)
```

**LoRA Configuration (from `FastLlamaModel.get_peft_model()` in llama.py):**
```python
lora_config = LoraConfig(
    r                   = 16,                    # Rank (default)
    lora_alpha          = 16,                    # Alpha (default, scaling = alpha/r)
    target_modules      = ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
                           "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"],
    lora_dropout        = 0.0,
    bias                = "none",
    task_type           = TaskType.CAUSAL_LM,
    layers_to_transform = None,                 # All layers by default
    init_lora_weights   = True,
    loftq_config        = None,
    use_rslora          = False,
    modules_to_save     = None,
)
```

**QAT Support:** LoRA weights can have `weight_fake_quantizer` attributes for Quantization-Aware Training (QAT)

---

### 4. EXISTING EXPORT & SAVE METHODS

**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py` (2678 lines) - CORE FILE

**Main Export Functions:**

#### A. Standard LoRA Save
```python
unsloth_save_model(
    model, tokenizer,
    save_directory,
    save_method="lora",        # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub=False,
    ...
)
```
- Saves only LoRA adapters using PEFT's standard format
- **Fastest option** - saves small files (~50MB)

#### B. Merged Model Save
```python
unsloth_save_model(
    model, tokenizer,
    save_directory,
    save_method="merged_16bit",  # or "merged_4bit"
    ...
)
```
- Merges LoRA weights into base model weights
- **"merged_16bit"** - merges to float16 (needed for GGUF)
- **"merged_4bit"** - keeps 4-bit quantization
- Slower but produces complete models

#### C. GGUF Export
```python
model.save_pretrained_gguf(
    directory,
    quantization_method="fast_quantized",  # ["q4_k_m", "q5_k_m", "q8_0", etc.]
    first_conversion="f16",
)

model.push_to_hub_gguf(repo_id, quantization_method=...)
```
- Converts merged 16-bit model to GGUF format (llama.cpp)
- Supports multiple quantization methods
- **Supported formats:** f32, f16, bf16, q8_0, q4_k_m, q5_k_m, q2_k, q3_k variants, q6_k, etc.

#### D. GGML Export (LoRA-specific)
```python
model.save_pretrained_ggml(save_directory, tokenizer)
model.push_to_hub_ggml(repo_id, tokenizer)
```
- Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format for llama.cpp
- Uses `llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py`

#### E. Merged Model with Inference
```python
model.save_pretrained_merged(directory, tokenizer)
model.push_to_hub_merged(repo_id, tokenizer)
```
- Generic merged save (delegates to appropriate format)

#### F. TorchAO Quantization
```python
model.save_pretrained_torchao(...)
```

**All methods added dynamically via `patch_saving_functions()` in save.py:**
```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False):
    # Adds these methods to model:
    model.push_to_hub_merged           # Merged model to HF hub
    model.save_pretrained_merged       # Merged model locally
    model.push_to_hub_gguf             # GGUF to HF hub
    model.save_pretrained_gguf         # GGUF locally
    model.save_pretrained_torchao      # TorchAO format
    model.push_to_hub_ggml             # GGML LoRA to HF hub
    model.save_pretrained_ggml         # GGML LoRA locally
```

---

### 5. KEY INTERNAL UTILITIES

**From `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`:**
- `get_lora_parameters(proj)` - Extract LoRA A, B, and scale from a layer
- `get_lora_parameters_bias(proj)` - Same + bias
- `fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)` - Dequantize 4-bit weights
- `QUANT_STATE(W)` - Get quantization state from weight

**From `/unsloth/models/llama.py`:**
- `FastLlamaModel.get_peft_model()` - Create PEFT LoRA config and wrap model
- `FastLlamaModel.from_pretrained()` - Load model with LoRA

**From `/unsloth/save.py`:**
- `_merge_lora(layer, name)` - Merge LoRA into base weights
- `fast_save_pickle(shard, name)` - Efficient saving
- `check_if_sentencepiece_model()` - Detect tokenizer type
- `upload_to_huggingface()` - Handle HF Hub uploads
- `create_huggingface_repo()` - Create repo on HF Hub

---

### 6. SAVE/EXPORT FLOW

**Standard Training Flow:**
```
1. Load model: model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(...)
   → Model already has LoRA applied via PEFT
   
2. patch_saving_functions(model) called in from_pretrained
   → Adds save_pretrained_merged, save_pretrained_gguf, etc.
   
3. Train model with SFT/DPO/RL trainer
   
4. Save options:
   - model.save_pretrained(..., save_method="lora")        # LoRA only
   - model.save_pretrained_merged(directory)                # Merged 16-bit
   - model.save_pretrained_gguf(directory)                  # GGUF for llama.cpp
   - model.push_to_hub_gguf("username/repo")               # GGUF to HF Hub
```

**GGUF Conversion Process:**
1. First: Merge LoRA into base model (16-bit float)
2. Install llama.cpp if not present
3. Convert HF format to GGUF (using `convert-hf-to-gguf.py`)
4. Quantize GGUF (using `llama-quantize`)

---

### 7. QUANTIZATION & MERGED WEIGHT LOGIC

**From `save.py` line 159-187 (`_merge_lora()`):**

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    """Merges LoRA weights into base weights for saving"""
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
    
    if quant_state is not None:
        # Dequantize 4-bit weights
        dtype = quant_state.dtype
        W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
    else:
        dtype = W.dtype
    
    W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
    
    if A is not None:
        # Merge: W += alpha/rank * A.T @ B.T
        W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
    
    W = W.t().to(dtype)
    return W, bias
```

---

### 8. MODEL-SPECIFIC TARGETS

**Default target_modules by model (from llama.py):**
```python
target_modules = ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
                  "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"]
```

These correspond to:
- Q, K, V, O projections in attention
- Gate, Up, Down projections in MLP

**Optional modules_to_save (learnable non-LoRA modules):**
- `embed_tokens` - embedding layer
- `lm_head` - output head

---

### 9. KEY FILES REFERENCE

| File | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| `save.py` | 2678 | ALL save/export methods, GGUF, GGML, HF Hub upload |
| `kernels/utils.py` | 800+ | `get_lora_parameters*()`, dequantize, matmul_lora |
| `kernels/fast_lora.py` | 500+ | LoRA computation kernels (MLP, QKV, attention) |
| `models/llama.py` | 3036 | FastLlamaModel, LoRA config, model patching |
| `models/loader.py` | 1000+ | FastLanguageModel factory, model routing |
| `models/_utils.py` | 1600+ | General model utilities, patching |
| `trainer.py` | 233 | Training wrappers, gradient accumulation fixes |

---

### 10. PEFT INTEGRATION

Unsloth uses PEFT library's `LoraConfig` and `PeftModelForCausalLM` for managing LoRA:

**PEFT Attributes on Models:**
- `model.peft_config["default"]` - LoRA configuration dict
- `model.peft_config["default"].to_dict()` - Convert to dict for inspection
- `model.add_model_tags(["unsloth"])` - Add metadata tags

**Available in peft_config dict:**
- `r` - rank
- `lora_alpha` - alpha (scaling)
- `target_modules` - which layers get LoRA
- `lora_dropout` - dropout rate
- `bias` - bias handling ("none", "all", "lora_only")
- `modules_to_save` - non-LoRA trainable modules
- `loftq_config` - LoFT-Q quantization config
- `use_rslora` - Rank-Stabilized LoRA
- `init_lora_weights` - initialization method

This is a complete and thorough exploration of the Unsloth codebase's structure, model classes, LoRA representation, and export mechanisms.

**From `/unsloth/save.py`:**
- `_merge_lora(layer, name)` - Merge LoRA into base weights
- `fast_save_pickle(shard, name)` - Efficient saving
- `check_if_sentencepiece_model()` - Detect tokenizer type
- `upload_to_huggingface()` - Handle HF Hub uploads
- `create_huggingface_repo()` - Create repo on HF Hub

---

### 6. SAVE/EXPORT FLOW

**Standard Training Flow:**
```
1. Load model: model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(...)
   → Model already has LoRA applied via PEFT
   
2. patch_saving_functions(model) called in from_pretrained
   → Adds save_pretrained_merged, save_pretrained_gguf, etc.
   
3. Train model with SFT/DPO/RL trainer
   
4. Save options:
   - model.save_pretrained(..., save_method="lora")        # LoRA only
   - model.save_pretrained_merged(directory)                # Merged 16-bit
   - model.save_pretrained_gguf(directory)                  # GGUF for llama.cpp
   - model.push_to_hub_gguf("username/repo")               # GGUF to HF Hub
```

**GGUF Conversion Process:**
1. First: Merge LoRA into base model (16-bit float)
2. Install llama.cpp if not present
3. Convert HF format to GGUF (using `convert-hf-to-gguf.py`)
4. Quantize GGUF (using `llama-quantize`)

---

### 7. QUANTIZATION & MERGED WEIGHT LOGIC

**From `save.py` line 159-187 (`_merge_lora()`):**

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    """Merges LoRA weights into base weights for saving"""
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
    
    if quant_state is not None:
        # Dequantize 4-bit weights
        dtype = quant_state.dtype
        W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
    else:
        dtype = W.dtype
    
    W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
    
    if A is not None:
        # Merge: W += alpha/rank * A.T @ B.T
        W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
    
    W = W.t().to(dtype)
    return W, bias
```

---

### 8. MODEL-SPECIFIC TARGETS

**Default target_modules by model (from llama.py):**
```python
target_modules = ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
                  "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"]
```

These correspond to:
- Q, K, V, O projections in attention
- Gate, Up, Down projections in MLP

**Optional modules_to_save (learnable non-LoRA modules):**
- `embed_tokens` - embedding layer
- `lm_head` - output head

---

### 9. KEY FILES REFERENCE

| File | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| `save.py` | 2678 | ALL save/export methods, GGUF, GGML, HF Hub upload |
| `kernels/utils.py` | 800+ | `get_lora_parameters*()`, dequantize, matmul_lora |
| `kernels/fast_lora.py` | 500+ | LoRA computation kernels (MLP, QKV, attention) |
| `models/llama.py` | 3036 | FastLlamaModel, LoRA config, model patching |
| `models/loader.py` | 1000+ | FastLanguageModel factory, model routing |
| `models/_utils.py` | 1600+ | General model utilities, patching |
| `trainer.py` | 233 | Training wrappers, gradient accumulation fixes |

---

### 10. PEFT INTEGRATION

Unsloth uses PEFT library's `LoraConfig` and `PeftModelForCausalLM` for managing LoRA:

**PEFT Attributes on Models:**
- `model.peft_config["default"]` - LoRA configuration dict
- `model.peft_config["default"].to_dict()` - Convert to dict for inspection
- `model.add_model_tags(["unsloth"])` - Add metadata tags

**Available in peft_config dict:**
- `r` - rank
- `lora_alpha` - alpha (scaling)
- `target_modules` - which layers get LoRA
- `lora_dropout` - dropout rate
- `bias` - bias handling ("none", "all", "lora_only")
- `modules_to_save` - non-LoRA trainable modules
- `loftq_config` - LoFT-Q quantization config
- `use_rslora` - Rank-Stabilized LoRA
- `init_lora_weights` - initialization method

This is a complete and thorough exploration of the Unsloth codebase's structure, model classes, LoRA representation, and export mechanisms.

gc.collect()
pass


def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

    pass
pass

    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]

# Check environments

IS_COLAB_ENVIRONMENT  = "\nCOLAB_"  in keynames
IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT = "\nKAGGLE_" in keynames

)

    "input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm",
    "pre_feedforward_layernorm", "post_feedforward_layernorm",
    "self_attn.q_norm", "self_attn.k_norm",
)

def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
pass

@torch.inference_mode
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

    # Push to hub
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,

    # Our functions
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
):
    if token is None: token = get_token()

    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()

    if commit_description is None:
        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
    elif "Unsloth 2x faster" not in commit_description:
        commit_description += " (Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
    pass

    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
        )
    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
        save_method = "merged_4bit"
    pass

    save_pretrained_settings = dict(locals())
    for deletion in ("model", "tokenizer", "save_method", "temporary_location", "maximum_memory_usage"):
        del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
    pass

    # First check for a token!
    if push_to_hub:

def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
    )
pass


def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
pass


def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
    """
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)

def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion     : str = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
):
    """
        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.

        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
